---
title: "Data Cleaning"
format: html
---

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(here)
```


## Read data

```{r}
# Load region descriptions data
region_raw <- read_csv(here("Data/raw/region_descriptions.csv")) %>%
  clean_names()

# Load state names from flux file
state_raw <- read_csv(here("Data/raw/By_State/FRF_net_flux_by_State.csv")) %>%
  clean_names()

# Load carbon pools data
carbon_pool <- read_csv(here("Data/raw/carbon_pool_descriptions.csv")) %>%
  clean_names()

# Load carbon flux data
flux_raw <- read_csv(here("Data/raw/By_State/FRF_net_flux_by_State.csv")) %>%
  clean_names()

# Load carbon stock data
stock_raw <- read_csv(here("Data/raw/By_State/FRF_stock_by_State.csv")) %>%
  clean_names()
```

## Explore data

```{r}
# Check structure and column types of each file
glimpse(flux_raw)
glimpse(stock_raw)
glimpse(carbon_pool)
glimpse(region_raw)
```


## Clean data
```{r}
# Expand the list of states into one row per state 
# Region descriptions list all states in a single comma-separated string per row
# Splitting into one row per state so we can join region to each state individually and create the state entity
region_expanded <- region_raw %>%
  separate_rows(description, sep = ",\\s*") %>%   # split comma separated states
  rename(
    Region = region,
    State_Name = description
  ) %>%
  mutate(State_Name = str_trim(State_Name))

# Get unique states and join their region to create the state entity
# Using state name as the ID since no numeric ID exists in the source data
state <- state_raw %>%
  distinct(state) %>%
  rename(State_Name = state) %>%
  mutate(State_ID = State_Name) %>%
  left_join(region_expanded, by = "State_Name") %>%
  select(State_ID, State_Name, Region)
```

```{r}
# Rename columns to create the primary key of the carbon pool entity
# Pool_ID and Pool_Name use the same value because the source file has no separate ID
carbon_pool <- carbon_pool %>% 
  mutate(
    Pool_ID = carbon_pool,
    Pool_Name = carbon_pool,
    Description = description
  ) %>% 
  select(Pool_ID, Pool_Name, Description)
```


```{r}
# The raw flux data has one column per year, so it needs pivoting to long format
# Since R adds an "x" prefix to numeric column names, we remove it to get a clean integer year
# Foreign key columns are renamed to match the primary keys of the related tables
flux <- flux_raw %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = matches("\\d{4}$"),   #  Find the column names that end with a 4‑digit year
    names_to = "Year",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Year = as.integer(str_remove(Year, "^x"))  # remove the "x"
  ) %>%
 mutate(
  # Create a primary key for the entity based on the index of the row
    Flux_ID = row_number(),
    State_ID = state,
    Pool_ID = carbon_pools,
    Year = as.integer(str_remove(Year, "^x")),
    Unit = net_flux_unit
  ) %>%
  select(Flux_ID, State_ID, Pool_ID, Year, Unit, Value)

```


```{r}
# Same pivoting to long reshape as flux
# Stock covers 1990-2023, where flux only goes to 2022 per the metadata
stock <- stock_raw %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = matches(".*\\d{4}$"),   # match any column ending in a 4 digit year
    names_to = "Year",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Year = as.integer(str_extract(Year, "\\d{4}")),  # extract the year
      # Create a primary key for the entity based on the index of the row
    Stock_ID = row_number(),
    State_ID = state,
    Pool_ID = carbon_pools
  ) %>%
  select(Stock_ID, State_ID, Pool_ID, Year, Value)

```


```{r}
# Check pool names across all three tables. Mismatches can break foreign key relationships in the database
unique(flux$Pool_ID)
unique(stock$Pool_ID)
unique(carbon_pool$Pool_ID)
```

### Standardize carbon_pool pool names
```{r}
# The Pool names in carbon_pool don't match flux and stock due to inconsistent naming
# It's necessary to standardize carbon_pool to match the other 2 tables

carbon_pool <- carbon_pool %>% 
  mutate(Pool_ID = case_when(
    Pool_ID == "Total Forest Ecosystem/Total Woodland Ecosystem" ~ "Total Forest Ecosystem",
    Pool_ID == "Aboveground biomass" ~ "Aboveground Biomass",
    Pool_ID == "Belowground biomass" ~ "Belowground Biomass",
    Pool_ID == "Dead wood" ~ "Dead Wood",
    Pool_ID == "Soil organic carbon (SOC)" ~ "Soil (Mineral)",
    TRUE ~ Pool_ID
  )) %>% 
  # Add missing pools from flux
  bind_rows(
    tibble(Pool_ID = c("Soil (Organic)", "Drained Organic Soil"),
           Pool_Name = c("Soil (Organic)", "Drained Organic Soil"),
           Description = NA)
  )
```


## Write Output

```{r}
# Set output path and create folder if it does not exist
datadir_processed <- file.path(here("Data", "processed"))
dir.create(datadir_processed, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Export cleaned tables as CSVs for ingestion into DuckDB
write_csv(carbon_pool, file.path(datadir_processed, "carbon_pool.csv"))
write_csv(state, file.path(datadir_processed, "state.csv"))
write_csv(flux, file.path(datadir_processed, "flux.csv"))
write_csv(stock, file.path(datadir_processed, "stock.csv"))
```
